title: dianthus glacialis
intro: Dianthus glacialis, also known as Glacier Pink, is a flowering plant species within the carnation family, native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The plant's roots and flowers can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote digestive health and relieve stress.
